Metadata.title: mind — your Claude Code's memory
Metadata.description: Personal knowledge vault, versioned in Markdown + git, integrated with Claude Code via a native Skill. Open source, MIT, no extra server.

Hero.subtitle: mind is a personal knowledge vault in Markdown and git — where your facts, your decisions, and the rules for how you want to be helped live. You own every line, no black box and no extra infra.
Hero.copyHint: $ create your repo → point it to yours → setup · no signup
Hero.fallbackText: Couldn't copy automatically — select the block below (edit your repo's URL before running it):

ConceptSection.heading: Every new session, the same recap.
ConceptSection.description: Built-in memory exists — but it's opaque, and it resets the way you have to explain things. mind swaps that for a vault you can see and edit.
ConceptSection.withoutQuote: “remember I told you about project X...”
ConceptSection.withoutBullet1: Black box: you can't see or edit what got stored
ConceptSection.withoutBullet2: Locked to the product — doesn't follow you if you switch tools
ConceptSection.withNoteBody: App in production. Next: review authentication...
ConceptSection.withBullet1: Real Markdown, in your git — opens in any editor

FeaturesSection.eyebrow: why it's not just another notes app
FeaturesSection.heading: Four decisions, one vault.
FeaturesSection.ownershipDescription: Versioned, visible Markdown, editable outside Claude — nobody's black box.
FeaturesSection.infraDescription: No MCP server, no vector DB. Just a native Claude Code Skill reading git.
FeaturesSection.howYouWorkTitle: Stores how you work, not just what
FeaturesSection.howYouWorkDescription: Conventions, rules, how you want answers — not just loose facts about your life.
FeaturesSection.forkDescription: Pulls improvements from the template (git fetch upstream) without losing your content.

ProofSection.heading: It's not a concept. It's a real repo.
ProofSection.description: The setup command and the folder tree below are exactly what's in the repository today.
ProofSection.cloneComment: create an empty, private repo before cloning
ProofSection.skillDemoCaption: // the Skill loads only the right node, never the whole tree

HowToStart.heading: Works with what you already have.
HowToStart.description: It's not a plain git clone: you become the owner of your own repo from the first commit — and you still keep receiving updates from the template whenever you want.
HowToStart.step1Description: Empty and private — GitHub, GitLab, doesn't matter. No README or .gitignore.
HowToStart.step2Description: Clone the template and swap the remotes: it becomes upstream, yours becomes origin.
HowToStart.step4Description: claude in the terminal — the Skill handles the rest.
HowToStart.readmeLink: See the full step-by-step (with commands) in the README →

FinalCta.heading: Your memory, in your git, today.
FinalCta.description: Your own repo, one script, and Claude Code stops starting from zero.

NodeGraph.caption: click a node · example from the vault

CloneCopy.copied: commands copied — edit your repo's URL before running them

SetupCommands.step1: 1) create an empty, private repo first (GitHub, GitLab...) — no README/gitignore

mind.label: Mind
mind.caption: root index — just links and a one-line description, no heavy content.

projetos.label: Projects
projetos.body:
# Projects

- [hobby](hobby.md) — personal project, built in spare time
- [company](company.md) — own business, 2 active products
projetos.caption: index folder: each of your projects becomes its own node, linked from here.

tarefas.label: Tasks
tarefas.body:
- [ ] Renew driver's license by 07/30
- [ ] Call the dentist
- [x] Review the apartment lease
tarefas.caption: pending items centralized here — never scattered inside a project node.

estudos.label: Studies
estudos.body:
---
tags: [graphs, proof]
---
## Summary
Dijkstra: shortest path with
non-negative weights — O((V+E) log V) with a heap.
estudos.caption: free-form category — there's no fixed tree, you create whatever folders make sense.

pessoal.label: Personal
pessoal.body:
## Preferences
- Coffee: pour-over only, no sugar
- Current book: Sapiens
pessoal.caption: day-to-day facts — Claude always asks before storing something like this.

conhecimentos.label: Knowledge
conhecimentos.body:
## interactive rebase
git rebase -i HEAD~5

squash commits before the PR.
conhecimentos.caption: loose technical knowledge, not tied to a specific project.

hobby.label: Hobby
hobby.body:
---
tags: [mobile, backend]
---
## Current status
App in production, automated deploy.
Next: review authentication.
hobby.caption: project node — 2nd level deep, linked from Projects.

empresa.label: Company
empresa.body:
## Current status
Own studio, 2 products in production.
See also → tasks/company.md
empresa.caption: cross-links ("See also") create extra edges — the graph isn't just a star.
